title: Ozothamnus cordatus
taxonomy: Ozothamnus cordatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia. Within the Asteraceae family, Ozothamnus cordatus is classified in the tribe Gnaphalieae. The genus Ozothamnus is often referred to as the 'button daisy' or 'everlasting'. Ozothamnus cordatus is a key component of the Australian flora, often found in heathlands and shrublands.

intro: Ozothamnus cordatus, also known as the common everlasting, is a shrub-like plant native to Australia, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers that remain intact for long periods. The leaves and flowers of Ozothamnus cordatus have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, as well as their ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ozothamnus cordatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ozothamnus cordatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
